Author: mithro_
Content: @Matt G. (Mobius) - As @tnt says, you need to use the default pad ring if you want the CoB add-on, otherwise you are free to do what ever you want.

Author: mguthaus
Content: Ok. What about the first part of the question? How do we generate the other pad frames? Will we have to add our own slot configurations etc?

Author: mguthaus
Content: Was there a reason that we didn't use the max IO option for the CoB? It doesn't change the core area.

Author: 246tnt
Content: The COB cost is per-bond-wire, more bond wire is more expensive.

Author: mole99
Content:
If you go for the 1x1 slot, you can simply change the `PAD_*` variables in this config: https://github.com/wafer-space/gf180mcu-project-template/blob/main/librelane/slots/slot_1x1.yaml
However, you must ensure that the pad instances you are referencing exist in the RTL: https://github.com/wafer-space/gf180mcu-project-template/blob/main/src/chip_top.sv

Author: mithro_
Content: Some IO configuration experiments are displayed at https://mithro.github.io/gf180mcu-project-template/

Author: mithro_
Content: @Matt G. (Mobius) - As tnt said, the cost is a few cents per wire-bond, so too many IOs makes it more than the $1.50 USD budget
